channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump administration rolls back a key protection for imperiled wildlife
item.description: The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act. The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration finalized a rule Friday that changes how agencies enforce the Endangered Species Act. The administration narrowed the definition of "harm" under the landmark law.

(Image credit: Carolyn Kaster)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: This week in science: California wolves, the world's bugs, and the earliest quasars
item.description: NPR's science podcast Short Wave talks about why wolves are thriving in California, a new estimation of the world's insect species and the universe's earliest quasars.
item.content:encoded: NPR's science podcast Short Wave talks about why wolves are thriving in California, a new estimation of the world's insect species and the universe's earliest quasars.
item.dc:creator: Regina G. Barber

item.title: EPA proposes weakening heavy-duty truck pollution rules
item.description: The Trump EPA calls Biden-era rules for cutting pollution from heavy trucks "unworkable." The proposed changes have been celebrated by trucking groups and denounced by environmental groups.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump EPA calls Biden-era rules for cutting pollution from heavy trucks "unworkable." The proposed changes have been celebrated by trucking groups and denounced by environmental groups.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: A new kind of robot swims the seas and soars the skies
item.description: Inspired by diving birds, roboticists built the lightweight machines to move from water to air. The design may one day lead to robots that can monitor and sample the coastal ocean.
item.content:encoded:
Inspired by diving birds, roboticists built the lightweight machines to move from water to air. The design may one day lead to robots that can monitor and sample the coastal ocean.

(Image credit: Raphael Zufferey)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Extreme heat on Independence Day will be America's new normal, experts say
item.description: In cities across the U.S., parades were canceled and events were delayed because of the heat. Meanwhile, emergency rooms saw a high number of people with heat-related illnesses.
item.content:encoded:
In cities across the U.S., parades were canceled and events were delayed because of the heat. Meanwhile, emergency rooms saw a high number of people with heat-related illnesses.

(Image credit: Amid Farahi)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: A rare flower is in a superbloom in the Rocky Mountains
item.description: Green gentian wildflowers are in a major superbloom in the alpine meadows of Colorado. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to David Inouye, a researcher who has been studying these magnificent flowers for decades.
item.content:encoded: Green gentian wildflowers are in a major superbloom in the alpine meadows of Colorado. NPR's Ayesha Rascoe talks to David Inouye, a researcher who has been studying these magnificent flowers for decades.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Scientists are using 'superworms' as a safer, efficient way to clean animal skeletons
item.description: To clean animal skeletons used for research and education, scientists are turning to a new type of beetle larva. These "superworms" provide an efficient solution to a difficult challenge.
item.content:encoded: To clean animal skeletons used for research and education, scientists are turning to a new type of beetle larva. These "superworms" provide an efficient solution to a difficult challenge.
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Researchers have created a synthetic cell that can adapt and learn survival skills
item.description: Scientists have created the most advanced synthetic cell yet made in a laboratory. They've dubbed it "Spudcell," echoing Sputnik, the first artificial satellite.
item.content:encoded: Scientists have created the most advanced synthetic cell yet made in a laboratory. They've dubbed it "Spudcell," echoing Sputnik, the first artificial satellite.
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Scientists volunteer their time to document biodiversity in the Smokey Mountains
item.description: Volunteer scientist spend their time tracking living things in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park. It's all part of an important science project.
item.content:encoded: Volunteer scientist spend their time tracking living things in the Great Smoky Mountains National Park. It's all part of an important science project.
item.dc:creator: Katie Myers

item.title: Lone star ticks are covering much of the U.S. Here's what you need to know
item.description: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.
item.content:encoded: It's a tick that hunts you down and transmits a potentially dangerous allergy to red meat. The New Yorker writer Burkhard Bilger discusses the lone star tick and the risks of alpha-gal syndrome.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross
